_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder for embedded PDF viewer

title: Command Line Basics
subtitle: Navigate, Create, and Manage Files Like a Pro!
overview: Students learn how to use the command line interface (CLI), open a terminal, navigate folders, create files and directories, and execute basic commands. The session provides hands-on experience with essential terminal operations used by programmers and system administrators.

images._note: Add screenshots of terminal windows, folder navigation, and command demonstrations.

keyTopics: clear command to clean terminal screen

youtubeVideos.title: Command Line Basics for Beginners
youtubeVideos.desc: Introduction to terminal commands and navigation.

youtubeVideos.title: CLI vs GUI Explained
youtubeVideos.desc: Understanding the difference between graphical and command interfaces.

activity.title: Terminal Navigation Challenge
parts: Windows Command Prompt / PowerShell / Linux Terminal
steps: Use pwd to display the current directory
Use ls or dir to list files
Create a folder using mkdir MyProject
Move into the folder using cd MyProject
Create a file using touch notes.txt
Return to the previous folder using cd ..
Clear the screen using clear or cls
Discuss where command line is used in real projects

quiz.explanation: CLI stands for Command Line Interface.

quiz.q: Which command shows your current directory?
Which command lists files and folders?
quiz.explanation: ls displays the contents of a directory.

quiz.q: Which command is used to change folders?
Which command creates an empty file in Linux?
quiz.explanation: touch is commonly used to create files.
cd .. moves one level up in the folder structure.

quiz.q: Which command clears the terminal screen?
quiz.explanation: clear removes previous text from the terminal display.

quiz.q: Which interface uses icons and windows?
quiz.explanation: GUI stands for Graphical User Interface.

quiz.q: Why do programmers use the command line?
options: It is faster and powerful for many tasks
quiz.explanation: Many development and automation tasks are faster using the command line.
